{{Short description|Japanese mythological deities}}
[[File:Japanese Primordial Deities-en.svg|frame]]
In [[Japanese mythology]], the {{nihongo|'''Kamiyo-nanayo'''|神世七代|1=Kamiyonanayo|2=ja|3=神世七代||lit. "Seven Generations of the [[Age of the Gods]]"}} are the seven generations of ''[[kami]]'' that emerged after the formation of heaven and earth.<ref name="kojiki-p72">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|p=72}}</ref>

According to the [[Kojiki]], these deities appeared after the [[Kotoamatsukami]], which appeared at the time of the creation of the universe. The first two generations were [[:en:Hitorigami|Hitorigami]] while the five that followed came into being as male-female pairs of ''kami'': male deities and sisters that were at the same time married couples. In total the ''Kamiyonanayo'' consist of 12 deities in this chronicle.<ref name="kojiki-p72"/>

In contrast, the chronicle [[Nihon Shoki]], points out that this group was the first to appear after the creation of heaven and earth. It also states that the first three generations of deities were ''hitorigami'' and that the other generations of deities were pairs of the opposite sex. Finally the Nihon Shoki uses a different spelling for the names of all deities.

The last generation formed by [[Izanagi]] and [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]] were the couple that would be responsible for the creation of the [[Japanese archipelago]] (''[[Kuniumi]]'') and would engender other deities (''[[Kamiumi]]'').<ref name="kojiki-p75">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|p=75}}</ref><ref name="kojiki-p77">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|p=77}}</ref>

{{Short description|Japanese mythological deities}}
[[File:Japanese Primordial Deities-en.svg|frame]]
In [[Japanese mythology]], the {{nihongo|'''Kamiyo-nanayo'''|神世七代|1=Kamiyonanayo|2=ja|3=神世七代||lit. "Seven Generations of the [[Age of the Gods]]"}} are the seven generations of ''[[kami]]'' that emerged after the formation of heaven and earth.<ref name="kojiki-p72">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|p=72}}</ref>

According to the [[Kojiki]], these deities appeared after the [[Kotoamatsukami]], which appeared at the time of the creation of the universe. The first two generations were [[:en:Hitorigami|Hitorigami]] while the five that followed came into being as male-female pairs of ''kami'': male deities and sisters that were at the same time married couples. In total the ''Kamiyonanayo'' consist of 12 deities in this chronicle.<ref name="kojiki-p72"/>

In contrast, the chronicle [[Nihon Shoki]], points out that this group was the first to appear after the creation of heaven and earth. It also states that the first three generations of deities were ''hitorigami'' and that the other generations of deities were pairs of the opposite sex. Finally the Nihon Shoki uses a different spelling for the names of all deities.

The last generation formed by [[Izanagi]] and [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]] were the couple that would be responsible for the creation of the [[Japanese archipelago]] (''[[Kuniumi]]'') and would engender other deities (''[[Kamiumi]]'').<ref name="kojiki-p75">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|p=75}}</ref><ref name="kojiki-p77">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|p=77}}</ref>

{{Short description|Japanese mythological deities}}
[[File:Japanese Primordial Deities-en.svg|frame]]
In [[Japanese mythology]], the {{nihongo|'''Kamiyo-nanayo'''|神世七代|1=Kamiyonanayo|2=ja|3=神世七代||lit. "Seven Generations of the [[Age of the Gods]]"}} are the seven generations of ''[[kami]]'' that emerged after the formation of heaven and earth.<ref name="kojiki-p72">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|p=72}}</ref>

According to the [[Kojiki]], these deities appeared after the [[Kotoamatsukami]], which appeared at the time of the creation of the universe. The first two generations were [[:en:Hitorigami|Hitorigami]] while the five that followed came into being as male-female pairs of ''kami'': male deities and sisters that were at the same time married couples. In total the ''Kamiyonanayo'' consist of 12 deities in this chronicle.<ref name="kojiki-p72"/>

In contrast, the chronicle [[Nihon Shoki]], points out that this group was the first to appear after the creation of heaven and earth. It also states that the first three generations of deities were ''hitorigami'' and that the other generations of deities were pairs of the opposite sex. Finally the Nihon Shoki uses a different spelling for the names of all deities.

The last generation formed by [[Izanagi]] and [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]] were the couple that would be responsible for the creation of the [[Japanese archipelago]] (''[[Kuniumi]]'') and would engender other deities (''[[Kamiumi]]'').<ref name="kojiki-p75">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|p=75}}</ref><ref name="kojiki-p77">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|p=77}}</ref>

{{Short description|Japanese mythological deities}}
[[File:Japanese Primordial Deities-en.svg|frame]]
In [[Japanese mythology]], the {{nihongo|'''Kamiyo-nanayo'''|神世七代|1=Kamiyonanayo|2=ja|3=神世七代||lit. "Seven Generations of the [[Age of the Gods]]"}} are the seven generations of ''[[kami]]'' that emerged after the formation of heaven and earth.<ref name="kojiki-p72">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|p=72}}</ref>

According to the [[Kojiki]], these deities appeared after the [[Kotoamatsukami]], which appeared at the time of the creation of the universe. The first two generations were [[:en:Hitorigami|Hitorigami]] while the five that followed came into being as male-female pairs of ''kami'': male deities and sisters that were at the same time married couples. In total the ''Kamiyonanayo'' consist of 12 deities in this chronicle.<ref name="kojiki-p72"/>

In contrast, the chronicle [[Nihon Shoki]], points out that this group was the first to appear after the creation of heaven and earth. It also states that the first three generations of deities were ''hitorigami'' and that the other generations of deities were pairs of the opposite sex. Finally the Nihon Shoki uses a different spelling for the names of all deities.

The last generation formed by [[Izanagi]] and [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]] were the couple that would be responsible for the creation of the [[Japanese archipelago]] (''[[Kuniumi]]'') and would engender other deities (''[[Kamiumi]]'').<ref name="kojiki-p75">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|p=75}}</ref><ref name="kojiki-p77">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|p=77}}</ref>

{{Short description|Japanese mythological deities}}
[[File:Japanese Primordial Deities-en.svg|frame]]
In [[Japanese mythology]], the {{nihongo|'''Kamiyo-nanayo'''|神世七代|1=Kamiyonanayo|2=ja|3=神世七代||lit. "Seven Generations of the [[Age of the Gods]]"}} are the seven generations of ''[[kami]]'' that emerged after the formation of heaven and earth.<ref name="kojiki-p72">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|p=72}}</ref>

According to the [[Kojiki]], these deities appeared after the [[Kotoamatsukami]], which appeared at the time of the creation of the universe. The first two generations were [[:en:Hitorigami|Hitorigami]] while the five that followed came into being as male-female pairs of ''kami'': male deities and sisters that were at the same time married couples. In total the ''Kamiyonanayo'' consist of 12 deities in this chronicle.<ref name="kojiki-p72"/>

In contrast, the chronicle [[Nihon Shoki]], points out that this group was the first to appear after the creation of heaven and earth. It also states that the first three generations of deities were ''hitorigami'' and that the other generations of deities were pairs of the opposite sex. Finally the Nihon Shoki uses a different spelling for the names of all deities.

The last generation formed by [[Izanagi]] and [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]] were the couple that would be responsible for the creation of the [[Japanese archipelago]] (''[[Kuniumi]]'') and would engender other deities (''[[Kamiumi]]'').<ref name="kojiki-p75">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|p=75}}</ref><ref name="kojiki-p77">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|p=77}}</ref>

{{Short description|Japanese mythological deities}}
[[File:Japanese Primordial Deities-en.svg|frame]]
In [[Japanese mythology]], the {{nihongo|'''Kamiyo-nanayo'''|神世七代|1=Kamiyonanayo|2=ja|3=神世七代||lit. "Seven Generations of the [[Age of the Gods]]"}} are the seven generations of ''[[kami]]'' that emerged after the formation of heaven and earth.<ref name="kojiki-p72">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|p=72}}</ref>

According to the [[Kojiki]], these deities appeared after the [[Kotoamatsukami]], which appeared at the time of the creation of the universe. The first two generations were [[:en:Hitorigami|Hitorigami]] while the five that followed came into being as male-female pairs of ''kami'': male deities and sisters that were at the same time married couples. In total the ''Kamiyonanayo'' consist of 12 deities in this chronicle.<ref name="kojiki-p72"/>

In contrast, the chronicle [[Nihon Shoki]], points out that this group was the first to appear after the creation of heaven and earth. It also states that the first three generations of deities were ''hitorigami'' and that the other generations of deities were pairs of the opposite sex. Finally the Nihon Shoki uses a different spelling for the names of all deities.

The last generation formed by [[Izanagi]] and [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]] were the couple that would be responsible for the creation of the [[Japanese archipelago]] (''[[Kuniumi]]'') and would engender other deities (''[[Kamiumi]]'').<ref name="kojiki-p75">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|p=75}}</ref><ref name="kojiki-p77">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|p=77}}</ref>

{{Short description|Japanese mythological deities}}
[[File:Japanese Primordial Deities-en.svg|frame]]
In [[Japanese mythology]], the {{nihongo|'''Kamiyo-nanayo'''|神世七代|1=Kamiyonanayo|2=ja|3=神世七代||lit. "Seven Generations of the [[Age of the Gods]]"}} are the seven generations of ''[[kami]]'' that emerged after the formation of heaven and earth.<ref name="kojiki-p72">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|p=72}}</ref>

According to the [[Kojiki]], these deities appeared after the [[Kotoamatsukami]], which appeared at the time of the creation of the universe. The first two generations were [[:en:Hitorigami|Hitorigami]] while the five that followed came into being as male-female pairs of ''kami'': male deities and sisters that were at the same time married couples. In total the ''Kamiyonanayo'' consist of 12 deities in this chronicle.<ref name="kojiki-p72"/>

In contrast, the chronicle [[Nihon Shoki]], points out that this group was the first to appear after the creation of heaven and earth. It also states that the first three generations of deities were ''hitorigami'' and that the other generations of deities were pairs of the opposite sex. Finally the Nihon Shoki uses a different spelling for the names of all deities.

The last generation formed by [[Izanagi]] and [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]] were the couple that would be responsible for the creation of the [[Japanese archipelago]] (''[[Kuniumi]]'') and would engender other deities (''[[Kamiumi]]'').<ref name="kojiki-p75">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|p=75}}</ref><ref name="kojiki-p77">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|p=77}}</ref>

{{Short description|Japanese mythological deities}}
[[File:Japanese Primordial Deities-en.svg|frame]]
In [[Japanese mythology]], the {{nihongo|'''Kamiyo-nanayo'''|神世七代|1=Kamiyonanayo|2=ja|3=神世七代||lit. "Seven Generations of the [[Age of the Gods]]"}} are the seven generations of ''[[kami]]'' that emerged after the formation of heaven and earth.<ref name="kojiki-p72">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|p=72}}</ref>

According to the [[Kojiki]], these deities appeared after the [[Kotoamatsukami]], which appeared at the time of the creation of the universe. The first two generations were [[:en:Hitorigami|Hitorigami]] while the five that followed came into being as male-female pairs of ''kami'': male deities and sisters that were at the same time married couples. In total the ''Kamiyonanayo'' consist of 12 deities in this chronicle.<ref name="kojiki-p72"/>

In contrast, the chronicle [[Nihon Shoki]], points out that this group was the first to appear after the creation of heaven and earth. It also states that the first three generations of deities were ''hitorigami'' and that the other generations of deities were pairs of the opposite sex. Finally the Nihon Shoki uses a different spelling for the names of all deities.

The last generation formed by [[Izanagi]] and [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]] were the couple that would be responsible for the creation of the [[Japanese archipelago]] (''[[Kuniumi]]'') and would engender other deities (''[[Kamiumi]]'').<ref name="kojiki-p75">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|p=75}}</ref><ref name="kojiki-p77">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|p=77}}</ref>

{{Short description|Japanese mythological deities}}
[[File:Japanese Primordial Deities-en.svg|frame]]
In [[Japanese mythology]], the {{nihongo|'''Kamiyo-nanayo'''|神世七代|1=Kamiyonanayo|2=ja|3=神世七代||lit. "Seven Generations of the [[Age of the Gods]]"}} are the seven generations of ''[[kami]]'' that emerged after the formation of heaven and earth.<ref name="kojiki-p72">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|p=72}}</ref>

According to the [[Kojiki]], these deities appeared after the [[Kotoamatsukami]], which appeared at the time of the creation of the universe. The first two generations were [[:en:Hitorigami|Hitorigami]] while the five that followed came into being as male-female pairs of ''kami'': male deities and sisters that were at the same time married couples. In total the ''Kamiyonanayo'' consist of 12 deities in this chronicle.<ref name="kojiki-p72"/>

In contrast, the chronicle [[Nihon Shoki]], points out that this group was the first to appear after the creation of heaven and earth. It also states that the first three generations of deities were ''hitorigami'' and that the other generations of deities were pairs of the opposite sex. Finally the Nihon Shoki uses a different spelling for the names of all deities.

The last generation formed by [[Izanagi]] and [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]] were the couple that would be responsible for the creation of the [[Japanese archipelago]] (''[[Kuniumi]]'') and would engender other deities (''[[Kamiumi]]'').<ref name="kojiki-p75">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|p=75}}</ref><ref name="kojiki-p77">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|p=77}}</ref>

{{Short description|Japanese mythological deities}}
[[File:Japanese Primordial Deities-en.svg|frame]]
In [[Japanese mythology]], the {{nihongo|'''Kamiyo-nanayo'''|神世七代|1=Kamiyonanayo|2=ja|3=神世七代||lit. "Seven Generations of the [[Age of the Gods]]"}} are the seven generations of ''[[kami]]'' that emerged after the formation of heaven and earth.<ref name="kojiki-p72">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|p=72}}</ref>

According to the [[Kojiki]], these deities appeared after the [[Kotoamatsukami]], which appeared at the time of the creation of the universe. The first two generations were {{ill|Hitorigami|en|Hitorigami|WD=Q3138614|lt=Hitorigami}} while the five that followed came into being as male-female pairs of ''kami'': male deities and sisters that were at the same time married couples. In total the ''Kamiyonanayo'' consist of 12 deities in this chronicle.<ref name="kojiki-p72"/>

In contrast, the chronicle [[Nihon Shoki]], points out that this group was the first to appear after the creation of heaven and earth. It also states that the first three generations of deities were ''hitorigami'' and that the other generations of deities were pairs of the opposite sex. Finally the Nihon Shoki uses a different spelling for the names of all deities.

The last generation formed by [[Izanagi]] and {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami}} were the couple that would be responsible for the creation of the [[Japanese archipelago]] (''[[Kuniumi]]'') and would engender other deities (''[[Kamiumi]]'').<ref name="kojiki-p75">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|p=75}}</ref><ref name="kojiki-p77">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|p=77}}</ref>

| {{nihongo|Omodaru|面足尊, Omodaru-no-mikoto}} and {{nihongo|Kashiko-ne|惶根尊, Kashiko-ne-no-mikoto}}

| {{nihongo|[[Izanagi]]|伊弉諾尊, Izanagi-no-mikoto}} and {{nihongo|{{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami}}|伊弉冉尊, Izanami-no-mikoto}}

{{Short description|Japanese mythological deities}}
[[File:Japanese Primordial Deities-en.svg|frame]]
In [[Japanese mythology]], the {{nihongo|'''Kamiyo-nanayo'''|神世七代|1=Kamiyonanayo|2=ja|3=神世七代||lit. "Seven Generations of the [[Age of the Gods]]"}} are the seven generations of ''[[kami]]'' that emerged after the formation of heaven and earth.<ref name="kojiki-p72">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|p=72}}</ref>

According to the [[Kojiki]], these deities appeared after the [[Kotoamatsukami]], which appeared at the time of the creation of the universe. The first two generations were {{ill|Hitorigami|en|Hitorigami|WD=Q3138614|lt=Hitorigami}} while the five that followed came into being as male-female pairs of ''kami'': male deities and sisters that were at the same time married couples. In total the ''Kamiyonanayo'' consist of 12 deities in this chronicle.<ref name="kojiki-p72"/>

In contrast, the chronicle [[Nihon Shoki]], points out that this group was the first to appear after the creation of heaven and earth. It also states that the first three generations of deities were ''hitorigami'' and that the other generations of deities were pairs of the opposite sex. Finally the Nihon Shoki uses a different spelling for the names of all deities.

The last generation formed by [[Izanagi]] and {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami}} were the couple that would be responsible for the creation of the [[Japanese archipelago]] (''[[Kuniumi]]'') and would engender other deities (''[[Kamiumi]]'').<ref name="kojiki-p75">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|p=75}}</ref><ref name="kojiki-p77">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|p=77}}</ref>

|associated_deities ={{ill|Izanagi and Izanami|WD=Q12259961}}; {{ill|Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|WD=Q5966692}}; {{ill|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|WD=Q478504}}; {{ill|Omodaru and Ayakashikone|WD=Q11152535}}; {{ill|Q11318771|WD=Q11318771}}; {{ill|Q4387158|WD=Q4387158}}; {{ill|Uhiji-ne and Suhiji-ne|WD=Q9090923}}; {{ill|Ōtonoji and Ōtonobe|WD=Q11073597}}}}

{{Short description|Japanese mythological deities}}
[[File:Japanese Primordial Deities-en.svg|frame]]
In [[Japanese mythology]], the {{nihongo|'''Kamiyo-nanayo'''|神世七代|1=Kamiyonanayo|2=ja|3=神世七代||lit. "Seven Generations of the [[Age of the Gods]]"}} are the seven generations of ''[[kami]]'' that emerged after the formation of heaven and earth.<ref name="kojiki-p72">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|p=72}}</ref>

According to the [[Kojiki]], these deities appeared after the [[Kotoamatsukami]], which appeared at the time of the creation of the universe. The first two generations were {{ill|Hitorigami|en|Hitorigami|WD=Q3138614|lt=Hitorigami}} while the five that followed came into being as male-female pairs of ''kami'': male deities and sisters that were at the same time married couples. In total the ''Kamiyonanayo'' consist of 12 deities in this chronicle.<ref name="kojiki-p72"/>

In contrast, the chronicle [[Nihon Shoki]], points out that this group was the first to appear after the creation of heaven and earth. It also states that the first three generations of deities were ''hitorigami'' and that the other generations of deities were pairs of the opposite sex. Finally the Nihon Shoki uses a different spelling for the names of all deities.

The last generation formed by [[Izanagi]] and {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami}} were the couple that would be responsible for the creation of the [[Japanese archipelago]] (''[[Kuniumi]]'') and would engender other deities (''[[Kamiumi]]'').<ref name="kojiki-p75">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|p=75}}</ref><ref name="kojiki-p77">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|p=77}}</ref>

|associated_deities ={{ill|Izanagi and Izanami|WD=Q12259961}}; {{ill|Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|WD=Q5966692}}; {{ill|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|WD=Q478504}}; {{ill|Omodaru and Ayakashikone|WD=Q11152535}}; {{ill|Q11318771|WD=Q11318771}}; {{ill|Q4387158|WD=Q4387158}}; {{ill|Uhiji-ne and Suhiji-ne|WD=Q9090923}}; {{ill|Ōtonoji and Ōtonobe|WD=Q11073597}}}}

{{Short description|Japanese mythological deities}}
[[File:Japanese Primordial Deities-en.svg|frame]]
In [[Japanese mythology]], the {{nihongo|'''Kamiyo-nanayo'''|神世七代|1=Kamiyonanayo|2=ja|3=神世七代||lit. "Seven Generations of the [[Age of the Gods]]"}} are the seven generations of ''[[kami]]'' that emerged after the formation of heaven and earth.<ref name="kojiki-p72">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|p=72}}</ref>

According to the [[Kojiki]], these deities appeared after the [[Kotoamatsukami]], which appeared at the time of the creation of the universe. The first two generations were {{ill|Hitorigami|en|Hitorigami|WD=Q3138614|lt=Hitorigami}} while the five that followed came into being as male-female pairs of ''kami'': male deities and sisters that were at the same time married couples. In total the ''Kamiyonanayo'' consist of 12 deities in this chronicle.<ref name="kojiki-p72"/>

In contrast, the chronicle [[Nihon Shoki]], points out that this group was the first to appear after the creation of heaven and earth. It also states that the first three generations of deities were ''hitorigami'' and that the other generations of deities were pairs of the opposite sex. Finally the Nihon Shoki uses a different spelling for the names of all deities.

The last generation formed by [[Izanagi]] and {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami}} were the couple that would be responsible for the creation of the [[Japanese archipelago]] (''[[Kuniumi]]'') and would engender other deities (''[[Kamiumi]]'').<ref name="kojiki-p75">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|p=75}}</ref><ref name="kojiki-p77">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|p=77}}</ref>

In [[Japanese mythology]], the {{nihongo|'''Kamiyo-nanayo'''|神世七代|1=Kamiyonanayo|2=ja|3=神世七代||lit. "Seven Generations of the [[Age of the Gods]]"}} are the seven generations of ''[[kami]]'' that emerged after the formation of heaven and earth.<ref name="kojiki-p72">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|p=72}}</ref>

According to the [[Kojiki]], these deities appeared after the [[Kotoamatsukami]], which appeared at the time of the creation of the universe. The first two generations were {{ill|Hitorigami|en|Hitorigami|WD=Q3138614|lt=Hitorigami|1=Hitorigami}} while the five that followed came into being as male-female pairs of ''kami'': male deities and sisters that were at the same time married couples. In total the ''Kamiyonanayo'' consist of 12 deities in this chronicle.<ref name="kojiki-p72"/>

In contrast, the chronicle [[Nihon Shoki]], points out that this group was the first to appear after the creation of heaven and earth. It also states that the first three generations of deities were ''hitorigami'' and that the other generations of deities were pairs of the opposite sex. Finally the Nihon Shoki uses a different spelling for the names of all deities.

The last generation formed by [[Izanagi]] and {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami|1=Izanami}} were the couple that would be responsible for the creation of the [[Japanese archipelago]] (''[[Kuniumi]]'') and would engender other deities (''[[Kamiumi]]'').<ref name="kojiki-p75">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|p=75}}</ref><ref name="kojiki-p77">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|p=77}}</ref>

|associated_deities ={{ill|Izanagi and Izanami|WD=Q12259961|1=Izanagi and Izanami}}; {{ill|Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|WD=Q5966692|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto}}; {{ill|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|WD=Q478504|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}}; {{ill|Omodaru and Ayakashikone|WD=Q11152535|1=Omodaru and Ayakashikone}}; {{ill|Q11318771|WD=Q11318771|1=Tsunugui and Ikugui}}; {{ill|Q4387158|WD=Q4387158|1=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto}}; {{ill|Uhiji-ne and Suhiji-ne|WD=Q9090923|1=Uhijini and Suhijini}}; {{ill|Ōtonoji and Ōtonobe|WD=Q11073597|1=Otonoji and Otonobe}}}}

{{Short description|Japanese mythological deities}}
[[File:Japanese Primordial Deities-en.svg|frame]]
In [[Japanese mythology]], the {{nihongo|'''Kamiyo-nanayo'''|神世七代|1=Kamiyonanayo|2=ja|3=神世七代||lit. "Seven Generations of the [[Age of the Gods]]"}} are the seven generations of ''[[kami]]'' that emerged after the formation of heaven and earth.<ref name="kojiki-p72">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|p=72}}</ref>

According to the [[Kojiki]], these deities appeared after the [[Kotoamatsukami]], which appeared at the time of the creation of the universe. The first two generations were {{ill|Hitorigami|en|Hitorigami|WD=Q3138614|lt=Hitorigami|1=Hitorigami}} while the five that followed came into being as male-female pairs of ''kami'': male deities and sisters that were at the same time married couples. In total the ''Kamiyonanayo'' consist of 12 deities in this chronicle.<ref name="kojiki-p72"/>

In contrast, the chronicle [[Nihon Shoki]], points out that this group was the first to appear after the creation of heaven and earth. It also states that the first three generations of deities were ''hitorigami'' and that the other generations of deities were pairs of the opposite sex. Finally the Nihon Shoki uses a different spelling for the names of all deities.

The last generation formed by [[Izanagi]] and {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami|1=Izanami}} were the couple that would be responsible for the creation of the [[Japanese archipelago]] (''[[Kuniumi]]'') and would engender other deities (''[[Kamiumi]]'').<ref name="kojiki-p75">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|p=75}}</ref><ref name="kojiki-p77">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|p=77}}</ref>

|associated_deities ={{ill|Izanagi and Izanami|qid=Q12259961|1=Izanagi and Izanami}}; {{ill|Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|qid=Q5966692|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto}}; {{ill|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|qid=Q478504|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}}; {{ill|Omodaru and Ayakashikone|qid=Q11152535|1=Omodaru and Ayakashikone}}; {{ill|Q11318771|qid=Q11318771|1=Tsunugui and Ikugui}}; {{ill|Q4387158|qid=Q4387158|1=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto}}; {{ill|Uhiji-ne and Suhiji-ne|qid=Q9090923|1=Uhijini and Suhijini}}; {{ill|Ōtonoji and Ōtonobe|qid=Q11073597|1=Otonoji and Otonobe}}}}

{{Short description|Japanese mythological deities}}
[[File:Japanese Primordial Deities-en.svg|frame]]
In [[Japanese mythology]], the {{nihongo|'''Kamiyo-nanayo'''|神世七代|1=Kamiyonanayo|2=ja|3=神世七代||lit. "Seven Generations of the [[Age of the Gods]]"}} are the seven generations of ''[[kami]]'' that emerged after the formation of heaven and earth.<ref name="kojiki-p72">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|p=72}}</ref>

According to the [[Kojiki]], these deities appeared after the [[Kotoamatsukami]], which appeared at the time of the creation of the universe. The first two generations were {{ill|Hitorigami|en|Hitorigami|qid=Q3138614|lt=Hitorigami|1=Hitorigami}} while the five that followed came into being as male-female pairs of ''kami'': male deities and sisters that were at the same time married couples. In total the ''Kamiyonanayo'' consist of 12 deities in this chronicle.<ref name="kojiki-p72"/>

In contrast, the chronicle [[Nihon Shoki]], points out that this group was the first to appear after the creation of heaven and earth. It also states that the first three generations of deities were ''hitorigami'' and that the other generations of deities were pairs of the opposite sex. Finally the Nihon Shoki uses a different spelling for the names of all deities.

The last generation formed by [[Izanagi]] and {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|qid=Q682306|lt=Izanami|1=Izanami}} were the couple that would be responsible for the creation of the [[Japanese archipelago]] (''[[Kuniumi]]'') and would engender other deities (''[[Kamiumi]]'').<ref name="kojiki-p75">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|p=75}}</ref><ref name="kojiki-p77">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|p=77}}</ref>
